name: Eleanor Crown
bio: Eleanor founded Herbert & Crown in 2011 after a decade at Carmody Groarke and David Chipperfield Architects. Her work is defined by an unflinching interest in structure as architecture — the idea that the thing holding a building up should also be the thing worth looking at. She leads cultural and civic projects across the UK and Middle East.

name: James Herbert
bio: James spent seven years at Herzog & de Meuron before returning to London to co-found the studio. His particular obsession is with the section — the cut through a building that reveals how spaces stack, light falls, and people move. He leads residential and commercial projects, and directs the studio's heritage practice.
awards: BD Architect of the Year Shortlist 2022

name: Priya Nair
bio: Priya joined Herbert & Crown in 2016 and leads all environmental strategy across the practice. She holds a BREEAM Accredited Professional qualification and advises on passive design, embodied carbon, and ecology across all project types. She is a visiting critic at the Architectural Association.

name: Omar Al-Rashid
bio: Omar leads the studio's Gulf operations from Dubai. His work focuses on the intersection of contemporary architectural language with regional climate and cultural context. He is currently project architect on the Dubai Cultural Centre and a mixed-use development in Riyadh.

name: Sarah Okafor
bio: Sarah works across residential and civic projects, with a particular focus on the social dimension of housing. Before joining Herbert & Crown she worked at Haworth Tompkins on community-led housing developments across London and Liverpool.
